name: builtin-types
description: MicroPython built-in type methods — what exists vs what's missing compared to CPython. CRITICAL for AI code generation: many CPython string/bytes/int methods do NOT exist in MicroPython.
verified_on_device: ESP32 MicroPython v1.28.0, via dir() on each type

missing_from_cpython.format_map: Like format() but takes a mapping dict
missing_from_cpython.isalnum: Check if all chars are alphanumeric
missing_from_cpython.isascii: Check if all chars are ASCII
missing_from_cpython.isdecimal: Check if all chars are decimal digits
missing_from_cpython.isidentifier: Check if string is a valid Python identifier
missing_from_cpython.isnumeric: Check if all chars are numeric
missing_from_cpython.isprintable: Check if all chars are printable
missing_from_cpython.title: Title-case the string
missing_from_cpython.translate: Translate characters using a mapping table

workarounds.maketrans: No direct equivalent; use str.replace() for simple cases

missing_from_cpython.isalnum: Check if all bytes are alphanumeric
missing_from_cpython.isascii: Check if all bytes are ASCII
missing_from_cpython.title: Title-case

missing_from_cpython.pop: Remove and return element at index
missing_from_cpython.title: Title-case

dict.note: MicroPython dict is insertion-ordered by default (since v1.19). No need for collections.OrderedDict.

frozenset.note: frozenset is available but no frozenset literal syntax (use frozenset([1,2,3])).

missing_from_cpython.bit_count: Number of ones in binary representation (Python 3.8+)
missing_from_cpython.bit_length: Number of bits needed to represent value

workarounds.bit_length: x.bit_length() works on MicroPython ESP32! (verify: int.bit_length not in dir but may work on instances)

int.note: MicroPython int is unbounded (like Python 3). Only from_bytes and to_bytes exist. bit_length() confirmed NOT available on instances either.

float.note: CRITICAL: MicroPython float has ZERO methods in dir(float). All float operations are via built-in functions (abs, round, int) or math module.

missing_from_cpython.itemsize: Size in bytes of each element

memoryview.note: MicroPython memoryview is extremely limited — only hex() method exists. Use bytes(mv) for conversion.

ai_code_generation_rules: NEVER use str.removeprefix() or str.removesuffix() — use startswith()/endswith() checks + slicing instead.
NEVER use str.casefold() — use str.lower() for case-insensitive comparison.
NEVER use str.translate() or str.maketrans() — use str.replace() for simple substitutions.
NEVER use float methods (float.hex(), float.is_integer()) — use built-in functions or math module.
NEVER use int.bit_count() or int.bit_length() — use bin(x).count('1') or verify bit_length() on instances first.
NEVER use bytearray.copy(), bytearray.reverse(), bytearray.pop() — use constructor or manual loops.
NEVER use memoryview.cast() or memoryview.tobytes() — use bytes(mv) instead.
For string padding: use format() rather than ljust/rjust/zfill: '{:>10}'.format(s) for right-align, '{:0>5}'.format(s) for zero-fill.
MicroPython has NO str.isascii(), str.isprintable(), str.isdecimal(), str.isnumeric(), str.isidentifier() — implement manual checks if needed.
